segments.text: Hello everyone, I'm Paul Giche and the CEO at Fabrics.
Today I'm going to show you how are we using the AutoDESKPS to build an M2N manufacturing automation system.
First of all, we're going to start with what is Fabrics and then we're going to show you three
different use cases on how are we using the AutoDESKPS API.
And finally, we're just going to ensure some of the results and the impact of what we are seeing.
So, what is Fabrics? Fabrics is an M2N platform for managing factories. We help
digitize factories from gold to the actual shipping of the part. That means that we are able to offer
quoting and we do have and one of this is going to be some of the topics we are going to discuss today.
We are based on different information that the clients provide. We're able to automate their
information. These information could be from obviously 3D files but also then all the
characteristics which different products they want to purchase from finishes and so on and so forth.
We do this pretty much on additive manufacturing, CNC, injection molding, sheet metal fabrication
and we also have some clients on the packaging sector. So, depending we call them configure
the different products that they offer and this is something that they can either use for their
internal team, for their commercial team or is it something that they can also offer to their
clients as a B2B platform or as we call it a customer portal platform. From there once they have
once the the quote is defined and the customer accepts the quote, pay we can capture the payment
if required then the manufacturing order automatically generated and then we are able to
orchestrate the production as says that do the quality assurance and reporting and also managing
the logistics to get the parts to the to the final customer. One of the first use cases I'm
going to explain is measure repair via API as you can imagine and this especially is very useful
in the wall of addedismo manufacturing. We do get our client the clients that we have that are
either service buddhas or corporates that are running an internal service buddha for their for
their companies and often they get broken files and that are not possible to be manufactured or
even be quoted in in some cases. Measure repair is a difficult topic and thanks to the auto-desk
automation API we are able to we are able to solve it. In this case as mentioned we do we do
force we do see different issues in broken measures this especially is the case on addedismo
manufacturing. On CNC we are not seeing this issue because in the CNC wall they would work with
step files or other formats that prevent this kind of issues but on the addedismo manufacturing
wall where fortunately or unfortunately STLs are still the norm and we do get off and
in our case we across the platform we see that roughly 20 to 24% of the files are broken or
need some repair and here we have from holes to non-manifold ages or self-intersection on some
of the polyons. The issue here is that manual repair is then consuming and require specialized
cut expertise not only so not everyone can do it in the company they need to have like a minimum
training and this sometimes means that requires back and forth internally between different teams
between the commercial team or between the commercial team and the technical team or the engineering
office and this could slow down the protein process or the validation process as well as it's
a protocol that maybe even is not even accepted and that's why what we do have is what we have
enabled is a misrepair workflow when a part is uploaded to fabrics we are able to
identify if that part is broken that it has like any of the issues mentioned before and then with
just one click we are able to send a file to the design automation API on Autodesk the file gets
repaired and then the file is returned for continuing the quoting process so and these are in the
clients that we have been testing it we estimate that they are saving roughly five hours per week
or manual work and that depends obviously on the volume that you're getting on on quotes but
if you have like a few tens of parts or even hundreds of parts per week this could this could
add up very quickly the amount of time that you are saving here then the other use case that we see
that that we are using the Autodesk API is for automatic build generation this is another case very
particular on the added manufacturing industry where when you have several of when you have several

when I mean manual is not doing the nesting manually is not adding the parts doing it's

when you are planning these several builds you need to juggle between order of priorities resource

what we are building in fabrics what we have is we do have a parts bug look
